text: The laparoscopic view reveals small bowel protruding from underneath the Roux limb of the gastric bypass. The surgeon inspects the abdomen to identify the anatomy and prepares to reduce the hernia through the Peterson defect, located between the mesentery of the large bowel, the mesentery of the small bowel, and the retroperitoneum.

The surgeon uses an endoscopic suturing device to close the hernia defect after reducing the small bowel. A braided permanent suture is applied starting as a purse string at the apex of the defect and continued as a running suture up to the border of the transverse colon.
